name: Whole-Wheat Sourdough Starter
recipeInstructions:
1. In a glass or ceramic bowl or jar that has been scalded, combine the whole wheat flour and active dry yeast.
2. Add the lukewarm water and stir well until combined.
3. Cover with plastic wrap and pierce with a fork to release gases.
4. Place in a warm, draft-free location at an even 85°F for 18-24 hours. Stir several times daily during this period.
5. Refrigerate until ready to use. If you have multiple starters, keep the whole wheat starter separate from others to preserve its distinctive flavor.
6. Whole wheat starter may not rise as quickly or as much as white flour starter; plan longer rising times if needed.
7. To replenish, always use whole wheat flour.
